specific_location: Private property along S bank of Arkansas River near Manzanola
description: Typical Black Phoebe. Petite all-black flycatcher except for a white belly. Pointed crest. Initially detected by hearing its repetitious, weak primary chirp song given nearby. It moved from a small to a much larger tree shortly after I found it, eventually perching on a very high twig at "11 o'clock' near the top of a 9-10-meter-tall plains cottonwood, along the bank of the rapidly flowing Arkansas River. It stayed there for many minutes. I even stopped back at my car to eat some breakfast, and went back to the tree and the bird was still in the same spot, singing. I thought about photographing or recording it, and would have if I'd known at the time it was that rare here.
